title: Venetian Carnival Duck Parade Designer
description: The user orchestrates a Venetian carnival parade by placing rubber ducks along a canal path, assigning each duck a unique venice mask, and customizing the parade with lighting, speed, and formation controls. Ducks wearing ornate masks march in formation while the user fine-tunes the spectacle through a multi-step configuration panel.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements a Venetian Carnival Duck Parade Designer. Load both shared_assets/venice_mask.glb and shared_assets/Duck.glb using GLTFLoader. Scene setup: Create a dark water-like reflective plane as a canal surface using MeshStandardMaterial with metalness 0.9 and roughness 0.1 in deep blue. Add ambient light intensity 0.3 and two colored point lights (warm gold and cool purple) flanking the canal. Set the camera at a 45-degree elevated angle looking down the canal with OrbitControls enabled. Core interactions requiring parameter validation: Step 1: A 'Spawn Duck' button clones a duck and places it at the next available parade position along a curved path. Validate that no more than 8 ducks can be spawned. Step 2: Clicking any duck in the scene (via raycasting) selects it, highlighting it with an emissive outline. Step 3: A 'Attach Mask' button clones the venice_mask model and parents it to the selected duck's head area, scaled and positioned appropriately above the duck. Validate a duck cannot receive more than one mask. Step 4: A color picker input lets the user tint the selected duck's mask material color. Validate hex color format. Step 5: A range slider labeled 'Parade Speed' (0.1 to 3.0, step 0.1) controls how fast all ducks bob up and down in a sine-wave animation. Validate the numeric range. Step 6: A dropdown with formation options ('Line', 'V-Shape', 'Circle') rearranges duck positions with a smooth tween using lerp over 60 frames. Step 7: A 'Toggle Spotlight' button adds or removes a spotlight that follows the selected duck, with intensity validated between 0.5 and 5.0 via an additional slider. Display a status panel showing total ducks, masked ducks count, current formation, and parade speed. All UI controls should be styled as a semi-transparent overlay panel on the left side.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  duckCount: number,         // total number of ducks spawned in the parade
  selectedDuckIndex: number,         // index of currently selected duck (-1 if none)
  maskedDuckCount: number,         // count of ducks that have masks attached
  paradeSpeed: number,         // animation speed multiplier for duck movement
  goldLightIntensity: number,         // intensity of the warm gold point light
  purpleLightIntensity: number,         // intensity of the cool purple point light
  canalReflectivity: number,         // metalness value of the canal surface material
  lastMaskColor: string,         // hex color of the most recently applied mask tint
  isParadeAnimating: boolean,         // whether ducks are currently moving along path
  maxDucksReached: boolean,         // true when 8 ducks have been spawned
}
